content:
# elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram Daily Update (Apr 29, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on April 29, 2026, focused on architectural cleanup and runtime stability. Efforts centered on resolving dependency coupling in the agent server, improving error handling for LLM provider configurations, and finalizing fixes for Telegram-related messaging flows.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS
*   **Agent Server Decoupling:** Successfully reduced hard dependencies on `app-*` packages within the agent server by migrating route registration to the plugin-lifecycle system.
    *   Refactored coordinator wiring and moved route logic into specific plugins ([#7204](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/issues/7204)).
*   **Runtime Error Handling:** Introduced a typed error class for missing LLM provider configurations to provide actionable feedback to users.
    *   Implemented `NoModelProviderConfiguredError` and updated the message service to short-circuit and provide hints when no model is configured ([#7203](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/issues/7203)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   None.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   **Architecture & Dependency Cleanup:** Resolved issues regarding dangling imports from deleted shim files and decoupled the agent server from specific app packages.
    *   [#7202](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/issues/7202), [#7204](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/issues/7204).
*   **Runtime & Messaging Improvements:** Addressed opaque error messages for missing LLM providers and finalized Telegram read-receipt logic.
    *   [#7203](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/issues/7203), [#7009](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/issues/7009).

## NEW ISSUES
*   **Build Configuration:**
    *   [#28](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/issues/28): Reported a discrepancy where the `package.json` declares a subpath export for `./account-auth-service` that is not being emitted by the current `tsup` configuration.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   None.
